case_name: Machine-account SYSVOL access = routine Group Policy processing (SMB-enumeration false positive)
security_proposition: The observed machine-account SYSVOL reads are adversary-driven SMB reconnaissance or lateral activity rather than routine Group Policy processing.

attack_category.category: Routine machine-account SYSVOL Group Policy processing
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = COUNTER. Machine-account read access to the two default Group Policy objects can resemble SMB reconnaissance, but the subjects, targets, and read-only pattern establish routine Group Policy processing. Do not leak to model.

A0.role_note: triggering alert: machine-account read access to the SYSVOL Policies path

EV-001.role_note: machine-account read of the Default Domain Policy gpt.ini

EV-002.role_note: machine-account read of the Default Domain Controllers Policy gpt.ini

EV-003.role_note: same-session machine-account read of the domain SYSVOL path

curation_notes: Counter (down_rank): machine accounts reaching the domain controller's \\*\SYSVOL share look like SMB enumeration / AD reconnaissance and trip lateral-movement detections.

Decisive counter-evidence (benign): the subjects are MACHINE accounts (NEWYORK$, SCRANTON$) and the targets are the well-known default GPOs under SYSVOL\...\Policies ({31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9} = Default Domain Policy, {6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04fB984F9} = Default Domain Controllers Policy) plus gpt.ini. This is routine Group Policy processing; correct verdict benign.
Cross-corpus twin of SMB-002 (attack_data machine-account SMB counter) — same benign-but-alarming surface from an independent corpus.

preprocessing_required: project raw fields to neutral attributes

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
